html:
[605]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 俄而 - Temporal adverb meaning 'soon after' or 'shortly.'
  • 冠珊瑚頂孔雀翎 - Describes a hat adorned with coral and peacock feathers, indicating a high-ranking official or dignitary.
  • 執手問訊 - A polite greeting gesture where one clasps hands and bows slightly.
  • 甚愛之 - Means 'very much loved' or 'treasured.'
  • 可惜 - Expresses regret or pity.
  • 始悟為鬼 - Means 'finally realized it was a ghost.'
  • 默不敢語 - Means 'silent and dared not speak.'
  • 詭請如厕 - Means 'deceptively asked to go to the toilet.'
  • 色若死灰 - Describes a pallid, lifeless appearance, often associated with the supernatural.
  • 松楸宿草 - Refers to ancient tombstones and overgrown grass, symbolizing a graveyard.
  • 詢之士人 - Means 'asked a scholar or literate person.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 珊瑚頂孔雀翎 - Symbols of high rank and status in imperial China, typically worn by officials or nobility.
  • 馬家坟 - Refers to the tomb of a military figure, likely a general who died in battle.
  • 馬将军 - A reference to a specific historical or legendary general who died in combat and was buried with his family.
  • 陣亡 - Means 'killed in battle,' emphasizing the general's heroic death.

Proper Nouns

  • 马将军 - The name of a historical or legendary general.
  • 马家坟 - The name of the tomb where the general and his family are buried.

[606]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 天廚星 - Celestial Kitchen Star; a celestial designation suggesting a special role or status.
  • 飲饌極精 - Extremely refined food and drink; emphasizes the high quality of the food and drink.
  • 督學蜀中 - Governor of Sichuan; indicates a high-ranking official position.
  • 乞董偕行 - Ask Dong to accompany; suggests a request for Dong to join the official.
  • 曹許之，遣董 - Cao agreed and sent Dong; shows the sequence of events.
  • 董不往，曹怒逐之 - Dong did not go, and Cao angrily dismissed him; describes the reaction to Dong's refusal.
  • 桃媚，天廚星也 - Taomei is the Celestial Kitchen Star; reinforces the celestial status of Dong.
  • 因公本仙官，故來奉侍 - Because you are originally a celestial official, so I came to serve; explains why Dong is there.
  • 督學凡人，豈能享天廚之福乎 - The governor is just an ordinary person, how could he enjoy the blessings of the Celestial Kitchen?; expresses the idea that only those with celestial status can enjoy such luxuries.
  • 爾來公禄将尽，某亦行矣 - Your good fortune will soon end, and I will also leave; is a warning about the impending end of Cao's good fortune.
  • 言畢，升空向去 - After speaking, he ascended into the sky and departed; describes the supernatural departure of Dong.
  • 不逾年，曹竟不禄 - Within a year, Cao died; indicates the fulfillment of the prophecy.
  • 夢中聯句 - Poetic couplets in a dream; refers to the poetic exchange that occurs during the dream.
  • 冉冉乘风一望迷 - Gradually riding the wind, one view is lost; is the first line of the poem.
  • 中天烟雨夕阳低 - Smoke and rain in the middle sky, the sunset is low; is the second line of the poem.
  • 来时衣服多成雪 - When coming, the clothes are mostly turned to snow; is the third line of the poem.
  • 去后皮毛尽属泥 - After leaving, the skin and fur are all turned to mud; is the fourth line of the poem.
  • 但见白云侵冷月 - Only see white clouds invade the cold moon; is the fifth line of the poem.
  • 何曾黄鸟隔花啼 - How often have yellow birds been heard singing through the flowers?; is the sixth line of the poem.
  • 行行不是人间象 - Step by step, it is not a human image; is the seventh line of the poem.
  • 手挽蛟龙作杖藜 - Hand-wrapping a dragon as a walking stick; is the eighth line of the poem.
  • 友山别去 - Youshan left; describes the departure of the dream companion.
  • 学士归语其妻，妻不答 - The scholar returned and told his wife, but she did not answer; shows the wife's lack of response.
  • 转呼仆，仆亦不应 - He then called the servant, but the servant did not respond either; shows the servant's lack of response.
  • 复坐北窗，取《椒山集》掀数页 - He sat again at the north window and picked up the *Jiashan Collection*, flipping through several pages; describes the scholar's actions upon waking.
  • 回顾已身卧竹床上 - Looking back, he found himself lying on a bamboo bed; describes the realization that it was a dream.
  • 大惊，始知梦也 - He was greatly surprised and realized it was a dream; concludes the dream narrative.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 椒山集 - Jiashan Collection; likely a collection of poems or writings attributed to a person named Jiashan, possibly a historical or literary figure.

[607]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 驚醒 - Literally 'startled awake,' used here to describe a sudden awakening.
  • 起視 - Means 'to rise and look at,' indicating an immediate action upon waking.
  • 宛然 - Means 'as if' or 'still,' suggesting that the book was left open in the same state as before.
  • 掀數頁 - Literally 'flipped several pages,' indicating that the book was disturbed.
  • 次日 - Means 'the next day,' marking the passage of time.
  • 碧眼見鬼 - A colloquial expression meaning 'seeing ghosts with blue eyes,' referring to the ability to see supernatural beings.
  • 自幼能見鬼物 - Means 'since childhood, able to see ghostly things,' describing the individual's lifelong ability.
  • 九歲，猶不言，尚記前生事 - '猶不言' means 'still not speaking,' and '尚記前生事' means 'still remembers past-life events,' indicating a unique childhood experience.
  • 能言後，不復記矣 - '能言' means 'able to speak,' and '不復記矣' means 'no longer remembers,' suggesting a loss of memory after gaining speech.
  • 午前猶不甚出，午后道路纷纷 - This clause describes the behavior of ghosts, with a clear contrast between morning and afternoon.
  • 率皆卑琐龌龊，無昂伟正大者 - This clause describes the general nature of the ghosts, requiring careful rendering to convey the contrast with noble and upright beings.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 胡公寶泉 - The identity of '胡公' and the meaning of '寶泉' are unclear, but '胡公' likely refers to a historical figure or a person of note in the context of the text.
  • 東岳大帝 - Refers to the God of Mount Tai, a major deity in Chinese folk religion, associated with the east and the afterlife.
  • 金将军 - Refers to a golden general, likely a deity or spirit associated with gold or wealth.
  • 狭面神 - Refers to a deity or spirit with a narrow face, described as unattractive.
  • 如来 - Refers to the Buddha, a central figure in Buddhism.
  • 仙子 - Refers to a fairy or celestial being in Chinese mythology.
  • 关公 - Refers to Guan Yu, a historical figure revered as a deity in Chinese folk religion.
  • 蒋侯 - Refers to Jiang Ziya, a historical figure revered as a deity in Chinese folk religion.

Proper Nouns

  • 胡公寶泉 - The identity of '胡公' and the meaning of '寶泉' are unclear, but '胡公' likely refers to a historical figure or a person of note in the context of the text.
  • 東岳大帝 - Refers to the God of Mount Tai.
  • 金将军 - Refers to a golden general.
  • 狭面神 - Refers to a deity with a narrow face.
  • 如来 - Refers to the Buddha.
  • 仙子 - Refers to a fairy or celestial being.
  • 关公 - Refers to Guan Yu.
  • 蒋侯 - Refers to Jiang Ziya.

[608]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 幼時過土地祠 - When I was young, I passed by a land temple.
  • 旁塑牛頭鬼 - There was a statue of a Niutou Gui (Ox-Head Ghost) nearby.
  • 鬼隨歸家 - The ghost followed him home.
  • 以角抵公臥床，震撼不已 - It used its horn to push against the official's sleeping bed, shaking it repeatedly.
  • 隨患疟 - He then fell ill with malaria.
  • 牛壓其胸 - The ox pressed down on his chest.
  • 太夫人祭之方去 - The lady of the house offered a sacrifice to it, and then it left.
  • 余答之曰 - I replied, saying...
  • 惟是神是佛，正直聰明，故知其為貴人正人而敬之 - Only the gods and Buddhas, being upright and wise, know that he is a noble and virtuous person and thus respect him.
  • 牛則無知也，何敬之有？ - But the ox is ignorant; how could it show respect?
  • 公撫河南時 - When the official was governing Henan Province...
  • 朔日行香 - On the first day of the lunar month, he went to offer incense.
  • 未至廟，忽低頭持扇遮面 - Before reaching the temple, he suddenly lowered his head and held a fan to his face.
  • 司道迎接打恭 - The officials of the province and the region greeted him with bows.
  • 岸然不答 - He stood upright and did not respond.
  • 公素謙 - The official was usually modest.
  • 一旦改常 - But on this occasion, he changed his usual behavior.
  • 司道大疑 - The officials were greatly puzzled.
  • 乘間問曰 - They took the opportunity to ask him, saying...
  • 得毋有所開罪乎？ - Did you not perhaps have some offense against us?
  • 非也 - No, that is not the case.
  • 前日見廟前有天蓬神兩位被河神鎖系 - The day before, I saw two Tianpeng gods in front of the temple being bound by the River God.
  • 求我說情 - They asked me to mediate on their behalf.
  • 我若允許，則彼原有罪 - If I agreed, then they would still be guilty of their original sin.
  • 如不允，則天蓬神纏擾不清 - But if I did not allow it, then the Tianpeng gods would continue to bother me without end.
  • 故佯為不見而過之耳 - Therefore, I pretended not to see them and passed by.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 土地祠 - A shrine to the Tǔdì (Land God), a local deity in traditional Chinese religion who protects the community and land.
  • 牛頭鬼 - A type of ghost or demon in Chinese folklore with the head of a cow, often associated with the Niutou Gui (Ox-Head Ghost), which appears in various Taoist and Buddhist texts.
  • 太夫人 - A respectful term for a high-ranking woman, often used to refer to the wife or mother of a notable person.
  • 朔日 - The first day of the lunar month, often associated with religious or ritual activities in traditional Chinese society.
  • 行香 - A ritual act of offering incense or performing religious rites, often at temples or shrines.
  • 司道 - Refers to provincial and regional officials in the imperial bureaucracy. 司 is 'office' or 'provincial administration,' and 道 is 'region' or 'district.'
  • 打恭 - A formal bow or greeting, used in traditional Chinese etiquette to show respect.
  • 天蓬神 - The Tianpeng (Heavenly Peng) gods are celestial beings in Taoist and Buddhist cosmology.

[609]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • ○龍母常熟李氏婦，孕十四月，產一肉團，盤曲九折，莹若水晶 - Describes a series of events and conditions, each clause building upon the previous one.
  • 惧，弃之河，化为小龙，擘空而去 - The sequence of actions is clear but requires careful rendering to maintain the flow.
  • 逾年，李妇卒，方殓，雷雨晦冥，龙来哀号，声若牛吼 - The sentence shifts from a temporal marker to a series of actions and descriptions.
  • 里人奇之，为立庙虞山，号“龙母庙” - The sentence combines a reaction, an action, and a result.
  • 乾隆壬午夏，大旱，牲玉既罄，卒无灵，桂林中丞以为大戚，其门下士薛一瓢曰：“何不登堂拜母乎”牲牢祷龙母庙，翌日雨降 - The sentence is complex, combining historical context, a problem, a suggestion, and a resolution.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 龙母 - A revered figure in Chinese mythology, often associated with dragons and water.
  • 常熟 - A city in Jiangsu Province, known for its historical significance.
  • 乾隆 - The reign title of Emperor Qianlong of the Qing Dynasty.
  • 壬午 - A specific year in the Chinese zodiac calendar.
  • 桂林中丞 - A high-ranking official in the Qing Dynasty, responsible for the region of Guilin.
  • 薛一瓢 - A historical figure, likely a scholar or official under the governor.
  • 龙母庙 - A temple dedicated to the dragon mother, often found in regions with significant water-related myths.

Proper Nouns

  • 龙母 - Dragon Mother.
  • 常熟 - Changshu.
  • 乾隆 - Qianlong.
  • 壬午 - Renwu.
  • 桂林中丞 - Governor of Guilin.
  • 薛一瓢 - Xue Yipiao.
  • 龙母庙 - Dragon Mother Temple.

[610]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 將至河間 - Indicates an impending action; 'as he was about to arrive at Hejian.'
  • 書一紙與河間人袁某 - Formal way of saying 'wrote a letter to Yuan, a man from Hejian.'
  • 道別緒甚款 - Conveys deep emotion in the farewell; 'expressed a very heartfelt farewell.'
  • 故老人所善 - Indicates a long-standing friendship; 'an old man who was a good friend of mine.'
  • 大驚 - Direct translation of the emotional state; 'greatly surprised.'
  • 即騎老人所贈黑马来迎 - Indicates immediacy; 'immediately rode the black horse given to him by the old man to welcome him.'
  • 小兒中道望見 - Means 'halfway' or 'along the way'; 'the child saw him halfway along the road.'
  • 抱袁腰白 - Physical action and 'said' or 'spoke'; 'embraced Yuan's waist and said.'
  • 別八年矣，猶相识否 - Indicates the passage of time; 'it has been eight years since we parted, do you still recognize me?'
  • 摩馬鬣笑曰 - Physical gesture; 'ran his hand along the horse's mane and said with a smile.'
  • 汝亦无恙乎 - Means 'safe' or 'well'; 'are you also well?'
  • 马为悲嘶不止 - Conveys the horse's emotional state; 'the horse neighed mournfully and would not stop.'
  • 道旁观者万人 - Hyperbolic expression; 'ten thousand people on the roadside.'
  • 皆呼生佛 - Metaphor for someone highly revered; 'all called him a living Buddha.'
  • 罗拜 - Collective act of reverence; 'worshiped in a circle.'
  • 渐长大 - Gradual process; 'gradually grew up.'
  • 纤妍如美女 - Describes physical beauty; 'as slender and beautiful as a young woman.'
  • 过琉璃厂 - Specific place name; 'passed through the Lufu factory.'
  • 见画店鬻男女交媾状者 - Direct translation of the term; 'saw a shop selling paintings of men and women engaged in sexual intercourse.'
  • 谛玩不已 - Indicates close examination; 'examined it closely and would not stop.'
  • 归过柏乡 - Passing through on the way back; 'returned through Baixiang.'
  • 召妓与狎 - Implies a close, intimate relationship; 'called a prostitute and engaged in intimate relations.'
  • 到五台山 - Specific place name; 'reached Wutai Mountain.'
  • 亲临观之 - Personal presence; 'personally witnessed it.'
  • 犹以为不足 - Continued belief; 'still thought it was insufficient.'
  • 更取香火钱往苏州聘伶人歌舞 - Temple funds; 'then took money from the temple and went to Suzhou to hire actors and musicians for performances.'
  • 被人劾奏 - Formal accusation; 'was accused and reported.'
  • 疏章未上 - Formal document; 'the petition had not yet been submitted.'
  • 老人已知 - Prior knowledge; 'the old man already knew.'
  • 无曲躬树而生色界天，误矣 - Metaphor for bending one's will; 'it is a mistake to bend the body and grow in the realm of desire.'
  • 端坐趺跏而逝 - Formal way of sitting in meditation; 'sat cross-legged and passed away.'
  • 年二十四 - Indicates age; 'at the age of twenty-four.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 河间 - Place name in Chinese history, likely referring to a specific region or city.
  • 袁某 - Proper name, likely referring to a specific person.
  • 琉璃厂 - Specific place name, likely referring to a market or area known for its crafts or goods.
  • 柏乡 - Place name, likely referring to a specific region or city.
  • 五台山 - Famous Buddhist site in China.
  • 苏州 - City in China known for its cultural and artistic heritage.
  • 生佛 - Metaphor for someone highly revered, similar to a living Buddha.
  • 色界天 - Concept from Buddhist cosmology, the realm of desire.

Proper Nouns

  • 河间 - Place name.
  • 袁某 - Proper name.
  • 琉璃厂 - Place name.
  • 柏乡 - Place name.
  • 五台山 - Place name.
  • 苏州 - Place name.
  • 生佛 - Metaphorical term.
  • 色界天 - Concept from Buddhist cosmology.

[611]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 吾友李竹溪与其前世有旧 - Suggests a spiritual or karmic connection, possibly implying reincarnation.
  • 见老人方作女子妆，红肚袜，裸下体 - Highly unusual and symbolic description.
  • 使一男子淫己，而己又淫一女 - Complex and ambiguous sexual act, possibly symbolic.
  • 其旁鱼贯连环而淫者无数 - Chaotic and orgiastic scene.
  • 李大怒，骂曰：“活佛当如是乎！” - Strong condemnation, possibly directed at the absurdity or impropriety of the scene.
  • 老人夷然应声作偈曰 - Composed and philosophical reaction.
  • 男欢女爱，无遮无碍 - Direct and unapologetic statement about sexual freedom.
  • 一点生机，成此世界 - Philosophical statement about the origin of life.
  • 俗士无知，大惊小怪 - Critique of the superficiality of common people.
  • 嗣后，登高临深，颇得如意 - Summarizes the character's improved abilities.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 李竹溪 - Fictional or historical figure, possibly a scholar or monk.
  • 活佛 - Term for a highly enlightened Buddhist figure, used ironically here.
  • 徐崖客 - Fictional character, possibly a wanderer or adventurer.
  • 孽子 - Term for a bastard or illegitimate child, often used in a derogatory sense.
  • 雁荡山 - Famous mountain range in Zhejiang Province, known for its scenic beauty and religious significance.
  • 僧 - Monk, a religious figure in Buddhism.
  • 皮囊 - Leather bag, often used for storage or as a protective covering.
  • 缠足布 - Piece of cloth used for binding feet, a practice associated with foot binding in Chinese history.

Proper Nouns

  • 李竹溪 - Fictional or historical figure, possibly a scholar or monk.
  • 徐崖客 - Fictional character, possibly a wanderer or adventurer.
  • 雁荡山 - Famous mountain range in Zhejiang Province, known for its scenic beauty and religious significance.

[612]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 入滇南 - Literally 'entering the southern region of Yunnan,' referring to entering the southern part of Yunnan Province.
  • 出青蛉河外千余里 - 'Traveling more than a thousand miles beyond the Qingling River,' indicating a long journey.
  • 迷道 - 'Lost one's way,' suggesting disorientation.
  • 砂砾渺茫 - 'Desolate and covered with sand and gravel,' describing a barren and desolate landscape.
  • 投囊野宿 - 'Seeking shelter in the wild by hiding in a bag,' indicating a desperate situation.
  • 声如潮涌 - 'The sound was like a tidal wave,' describing the loud and continuous noise.
  • 偷目之 - 'Peeping at it,' indicating a cautious and stealthy action.
  • 大毛人 - 'Big hairy people,' referring to large, hairy creatures.
  • 方目钩鼻 - 'Square eyes and hooked nose,' describing the physical features of the creatures.
  • 两牙出颈外数尺 - 'Two teeth protruding several feet from the neck,' emphasizing the size and ferocity of the creatures.
  • 长倍数人 - 'Taller than several people,' indicating their immense size.
  • 如万群獐兔被逐狂奔者 - 'Like thousands of deer and rabbits being chased and running wildly,' creating a vivid image of panic.
  • 俄而，大风自西南起 - 'Soon, a strong wind arose from the southwest,' indicating a sudden change in weather.
  • 腥不可耐 - 'The smell was unbearable,' describing the intense odor.
  • 乃蟒蛇从空中过 - 'It turned out to be a python passing through the air,' indicating the appearance of a large snake.
  • 驱群兽而行 - 'Driving the herd of beasts,' describing the snake's movement.
  • 长数十丈 - 'Several dozen zhang long,' indicating the enormous size of the snake.
  • 头若车轮 - 'Head like a cartwheel,' describing the size and shape of the snake's head.
  • 徐惕息噤声而伏 - 'Xu held his breath, remained silent, and lay down,' indicating a state of fear and caution.
  • 天明出囊 - 'At dawn, Xu emerged from the bag,' marking the passage of time.
  • 见蛇过处两旁草木皆焦 - 'Seeing that the grass and trees on both sides of the snake's path were scorched,' describing the aftermath of the snake's passage.
  • 己独无恙 - 'Only he was unharmed,' indicating his survival.
  • 饥无乞食处 - 'Hungry and with nowhere to beg for food,' describing his dire situation.
  • 望前村有若烟起者 - 'Seeing a village ahead where smoke was rising,' indicating the presence of a settlement.
  • 奔往 - 'Ran towards it,' showing his urgency.
  • 见二毛人并坐 - 'Seeing two hairy people sitting together,' describing the encounter.
  • 旁置镬，芋甚香 - 'With a pot nearby, steaming yams smelled very fragrant,' indicating the presence of food.
  • 徐疑即月下遗溲者 - 'Xu suspected they were the ones who had urinated on the skin bag at night,' linking the earlier event.
  • 跪而再拜 - 'Kneeled and bowed twice,' showing respect and gratitude.
  • 毛人不知 - 'The hairy people did not understand,' indicating a communication barrier.
  • 哀乞救饥 - 'Appealed for help to save him from hunger,' expressing his desperation.
  • 色态甚和 - 'Their demeanor was very gentle,' describing their reaction.
  • 睨徐而笑 - 'Glanced at Xu and laughed,' showing a playful or amused response.
  • 哑哑有声，响震林谷 - 'Made a sound like 'ya ya,' which echoed through the forest and valley,' describing the noise.
  • 若解意者 - 'As if they understood,' suggesting a possible connection.
  • 赐以二芋 - 'Gave him two yams,' indicating their generosity.
  • 徐得果腹 - 'Xu was able to eat enough,' showing his relief.
  • 留半芋 - 'Left half a yam,' indicating his gratitude and consideration.
  • 归视诸人，乃白石也 - 'When he returned and looked at the yams, they turned out to be white stones,' revealing the deception.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 滇南 - Southern Yunnan, a region in southwestern China known for its diverse ethnic groups and natural landscapes.
  • 青蛉河 - Qingling River, a river in Yunnan Province.
  • 毛人 - A term used to describe large, hairy creatures, possibly mythical or legendary beings.
  • 蟒蛇 - Python, a large snake known for its size and strength.
  • 镬 - A type of cooking pot.
  • 芋 - Yams, a common food item in traditional Chinese cuisine.

Proper Nouns

  • 滇南 - Southern Yunnan.
  • 青蛉河 - Qingling River.
  • 徐 - Xu, the protagonist of the story.
  • 毛人 - Hairy people.
  • 蟒蛇 - Python.
  • 镬 - Cooking pot.
  • 芋 - Yams.

[613]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 徐游遍四海 - Literally 'slowly travel around the four seas,' meaning to travel extensively and leisurely.
  • 仍归湖州 - Literally 'still return to Huzhou,' indicating the return to a specific place after a journey.
  • 荒莽幽绝之所 - Literally 'desolate, wild, and secluded places,' describing remote and uninhabited areas.
  • 鬼神怪物 - Literally 'ghosts, gods, and monsters,' referring to supernatural beings.
  • 有鬼神怪物处，便有人矣 - Literally 'where there are ghosts, gods, and monsters, there must be people,' implying that inhabited places are more likely to have supernatural elements than uninhabited ones.
  • 陕西兴安州民某 - Literally 'a certain person from Xing'an Prefecture in Shaanxi,' referring to an unnamed individual.
  • 六月娶妻 - Literally 'married in June,' specifying the time of the marriage.
  • 天大暑 - Literally 'the weather is very hot,' describing the climatic conditions.
  • 路远 - Literally 'the road is far,' indicating the distance of the journey.
  • 新妇以红巾裹首 - Literally 'the new wife wrapped her head with a red scarf,' describing the attire of the bride.
  • 不胜闷热 - Literally 'unable to bear the heat and stuffiness,' indicating discomfort due to the hot weather.
  • 暴死车中 - Literally 'died suddenly in the carriage,' describing the sudden death of the bride.
  • 其父母悲甚 - Literally 'her parents were very sad,' indicating the emotional state of the bride's parents.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '闽商陈某，与诸客泛海，遇飓风，飘至一山脚下' introduces the main characters and sets the scene, but it is elliptical and requires careful parsing to understand the sequence of events.
  • The clause '见山崖平坦可步，相率樵采' describes the initial reaction of the group, but the exact actions and motivations are implied.
  • The sequence of events in '初进，路甚仄，行一二里，即觉开旷' is described in a compressed form, requiring careful reading to understand the progression.
  • The clause '天色将暮，闻海风萧飒，林鸟啾啁，不敢深入，乃归' describes the group's decision to return, but the reasons for their fear are implied.
  • The clause '次日，风更甚，舟不行，舟中人悔昨未穷其境，约再往，拉陈与偕' introduces a new day and the group's decision to return, but the exact sequence of events is implied.
  • The clause '迹前径行八九里，有一溪，水色澄绿，旁有土山，不甚高，穴中似有物喘息' describes the group's journey and the discovery of the cave, but the exact sequence of events is implied.
  • The clause '众惧窜走，陈恃胆力，上在树隐身觇之' describes Chen's actions, but the exact sequence of events is implied.
  • The clause '食顷，其物出穴外，大倍水牛而形似象，顶生一角，晶莹犀利，盘踞石上长啸，声裂竹木' describes the appearance and actions of the creature, but the exact sequence of events is implied.
  • The clause '陈惊惧几坠，但见虎豹猿鹿各以其属至，俯伏其下，不止千计' describes the group's reaction to the creature, but the exact sequence of events is implied.
  • The clause '其物择肥者践之，用舌舐其腹，吸其血，百兽皆股栗不敢动' describes the creature's actions, but the exact sequence of events is implied.
  • The clause '食三四兽，复曳尾入穴' describes the creature's final actions, but the exact sequence of events is implied.
  • The clause '客乃下，寻旧径归，与众言所见，终未知山与兽何名也' describes the guests' return and their conversation, but the exact sequence of events is implied.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 太守方公 - Refers to a local official named Fang Gong.
  • 晋元康中 - Refers to the Yuan Kang period (290-291 AD) of the Western Jin dynasty.

  • 此名犀犬，得者其家富昌 - Rhinoceros dogs are a mythical creature, and the belief that possessing them brings prosperity is a cultural superstition.
  • 事载《异苑》 - *The Strange Realm* is a collection of strange and supernatural stories compiled by Liu Yiqing during the Southern Song dynasty.
  • ○旱魃 - The 'drought demon' is a mythological figure associated with droughts in Chinese folklore.
  • 乾隆二十六年 - Refers to the 26th year of the Qianlong era (1761 AD).
  • 京师 - Refers to Beijing, the capital of China.

  • 漏下出城 - Refers to the drum striking for the night watch, indicating the time when people were supposed to be inside their homes.
  • 黑风 - Black wind is often associated with supernatural or ominous events in Chinese folklore.
  • 邮亭 - A post station or rest stop along the road.
  • 健步 - Refers to a swift-footed or fast runner.
  • 都统 - A military rank equivalent to a general.
  • 公文 - Official documents.
  • 荒冢 - A deserted or abandoned grave.
  • 五十刻 - A unit of time, with one day divided into 100 kē. Fifty kē is approximately two and a half hours.

  • 病卒 - Literally 'died from illness,' commonly used to denote a person who died due to illness.
  • 忽蘇 - 'Suddenly revived' or 'suddenly regained consciousness,' often used in classical texts to describe a miraculous or supernatural awakening.
  • 小字金娥 - 'Small name Jin’e,' referring to a personal or pet name used by family or close friends.

  • 大恸 - 'Great grief' or 'deep sorrow,' used to describe intense emotional crying.
  • 素不識字 - 'Never knew how to read or write,' indicating a lack of education.

  • 能吟咏 - 'Able to recite poetry,' indicating a sudden literary ability.
  • 舉止嫻雅 - 'Behaviors refined and elegant,' describing a person's graceful demeanor.

  • 蒋府 - 'Jiang’s mansion' or 'the Jiang family estate,' referring to the residence of the Jiang family.
  • 病後忽識字 - A motif common in classical Chinese literature, especially in ghost stories and tales of transformation, where a ghost or revived person gains sudden knowledge or ability.
  • 吟咏 - The act of reciting or composing poetry, a hallmark of the refined and educated class in classical Chinese society.
  • 舉止嫻雅 - A phrase used to describe the demeanor of a cultivated or aristocratic woman, in contrast to a common village wife.

  • 笑郎以七尺男子，何难得五百金哉 - A rhetorical question, '笑郎' means 'laugh at you,' '七尺男子' is a traditional way to refer to a man of average height, and '何难得五百金哉' means 'how hard is it to obtain five hundred gold coins?'
  • 然则顷云‘有有’者即卿耶 - '然则' means 'then,' '顷云' means 'just now said,' '有有' is a play on words, and '卿' is an affectionate term for 'you.'

  • 戚徐姓者 - Refers to a relative of the surname Xu, indicating a familial connection.
  • 能持《金刚经》 - Means 'able to recite the *Diamond Sutra*,' referring to the practice of chanting a sacred Buddhist text.
  • 功德 - A compound term meaning 'merit' or 'blessings' earned through virtuous deeds or religious practices.
  • 阎罗殿 - The 'Yama Court,' the realm of the god of death in Buddhist cosmology, where souls are judged.
  • 奉上帝檄 - Means 'sent by the command of the Heavenly Emperor,' referring to a divine mandate or decree.
  • 硬向我要某司寇去 - Means 'forcibly demanded that I hand over the Grand Judge,' indicating a demand made with authority or force.
  • 地藏王 - Refers to the Bodhisattva Ksitigarbha, a revered figure in Chinese Buddhism associated with saving souls from hell.
  • 妄召尊神 - Means 'improperly summoning a revered deity,' suggesting a breach of religious protocol.
  • 罚减阳寿一纪 - Means 'punished by reducing one's lifespan by a *jī* (a unit of time),' referring to a divine penalty for improper actions.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 金刚神 - The Vajra, a Buddhist deity symbolizing indestructibility and protection, often associated with the *Diamond Sutra*.

  • 《金刚经》 - The *Diamond Sutra*, a central Mahayana Buddhist scripture emphasizing the concept of emptiness (*śūnyatā*).
  • 冥司 - The underworld or realm of the dead in Chinese cosmology, where souls are judged and punished.
  • 阳寿 - The lifespan granted to a person by the heavens, a concept in Chinese religion and philosophy.

  • 我虽有妒行，然罪无死法 - Phrase meaning 'although I have a jealous nature, my crime does not warrant death,' explaining the speaker's perceived lack of severe punishment.
  • 我夫不良，趁我生产时，嘱稳婆将二铁针置产门中，以此陨命 - Phrase meaning 'my husband was unscrupulous, taking advantage of my childbirth to instruct the midwife to place two iron needles in my birth canal, causing my death,' detailing a specific act of betrayal.
  • 一家之人，竟无知者 - Phrase meaning 'none of the family members knew about it,' indicating a lack of awareness.
  • 我诉城隍神，神说我夫阳寿未终，不准审理 - Phrase meaning 'I appealed to the City God, but the City God said my husband's earthly life had not ended, so he would not allow the case to be heard,' describing a failed appeal.
  • 前月关帝过此，我往喊冤，城隍说我冲突仪仗，又缚我放香案脚下 - Phrase meaning 'last month when Guan Di passed by, I went to appeal, but the City God said I disrupted the procession and tied me to the incense altar,' describing another failed attempt at justice.
  • 幸天网恢恢，我夫来烧头香，被我捉住，特来索命 - Phrase meaning 'fortunately, the vast net of heaven has its ways, and my husband came to burn the first incense, where I caught him and came to demand my life,' indicating a supernatural retribution.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The clause '一日，拉其友楊孝廉至三十里外，以情告之' is a narrative sequence that must be read as a single action.
  • The question '我記《東醫寶鑒》中有治狐術一條，何不試之？' is a suggestion based on the memory of a specific entry.
  • The phrase '遂偕往琉璃廠，' indicates that they went together to Lüliu Factory.

  • 几为石柱碰毙 - 几 means 'almost,' 为 means 'by,' 石柱 means 'stone pillar,' and 碰毙 means 'collide and die.' This phrase suggests that the brother almost died by colliding with a stone pillar.
  • 其时幸我在旁 - 其时 means 'at that time,' 幸 means 'fortunately,' 我 means 'I,' and 在旁 means 'beside.' This phrase indicates that the speaker was fortunate to be present at the time.
  • 使柱旁倒 - 使 means 'make,' 柱 means 'pillar,' and 旁倒 means 'fall over.' This phrase describes how the speaker made the pillar fall over.

  • 柱下有古冢一丘 - 柱下 means 'under the pillar,' 有 means 'there is,' 古冢 means 'ancient tomb,' and 一丘 means 'a mound.' This phrase describes an ancient tomb located under the pillar.

  • 使他枯骨日浸水中 - 使 means 'make,' 他 means 'it,' 枯骨 means 'dry bones,' and 日浸水中 means 'daily immersed in water.' This phrase describes how the dry bones were daily immersed in water.

  • 我再三求之 - 我 means 'I,' 再三 means 'repeatedly,' and 求之 means 'asked him.' This phrase indicates that the speaker repeatedly asked the dry bones.
  • 彼方允诺 - 彼 means 'he,' 方 means 'finally,' and 允诺 means 'agreed.' This phrase suggests that the dry bones finally agreed.
  • 八兄须为迁葬 - 八兄 means 'older brother,' 须 means 'must,' 为 means 'to,' and 迁葬 means 'relocate the burial.' This phrase instructs the brother to relocate the burial.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 此猿洞也 - Literal: 'This is the monkey cave.' Sets the scene and introduces the location.
  • 为虎所据 - Literal: 'Occupied by a tiger.' Describes the initial state of the cave.
  • 招余杀虎 - Literal: 'Invite me to kill the tiger.' Indicates an invitation or request.
  • 殪其类 - Literal: 'Kill its kind.' Refers to eliminating all tigers in the area.
  • 因得居此 - Literal: 'Therefore, I was able to live here.' Explains the consequence of killing the tigers.
  • 床置二剑，光如沃雪 - Literal: 'Two swords placed on the bed, their light as bright as melted snow.' Describes the appearance of the swords.
  • 台上供河洛二图、六十四卦 - Literal: 'On the platform, there are the two diagrams of the Yellow River and Luo River, and the sixty-four hexagrams.' Refers to important cultural and religious symbols.
  • 地堆虎皮数十张 - Literal: 'The ground is piled with dozens of tiger skins.' Describes the decoration of the cave.
  • 笑谓曹曰 - Literal: 'Laughed and said to Cao.' Introduces a dialogue.
  • 明日诸猿来寿我，颇可观 - Literal: 'Tomorrow, the monkeys will come to celebrate my birthday, it will be quite impressive.' Indicates a planned celebration.
  • 有数小猿至洞前，见有人，惊跳去 - Literal: 'Several small monkeys came to the front of the cave, saw someone, and jumped away in fright.' Describes the monkeys' reaction.
  • 自虎害除，猿感我恩，每日轮班来供使令 - Literal: 'Since the tiger's harm was removed, the monkeys are grateful for my kindness, and they come in shifts every day to serve me.' Explains the monkeys' behavior.
  • 我将请客，可拾薪煨芋 - Literal: 'I am going to entertain guests, you can gather firewood and cook yams.' Gives instructions.
  • 猿跃去，少顷，捧薪至，煮芋与曹共啖 - Literal: 'The monkeys jumped away, soon after, they brought firewood, cooked yams, and ate with Cao.' Describes the monkeys' actions.
  • 曹私忆此间得酒更佳 - Literal: 'Cao privately remembered that getting wine here would be even better.' Expresses a personal thought.
  • 酌而共饮 - Literal: 'Drank it together.' Describes the action of drinking the wine.
  • 老人醉，取双剑舞，走电飞沙，天风皆起 - Literal: 'The old man got drunk, took the two swords and danced, the lightning moved, the sand flew, and the wind rose.' Describes the old man's performance.
  • 舞毕还洞，枕虎皮卧，语曹云 - Literal: 'After finishing the dance, he returned to the cave, lay down on the tiger skin, and said to Cao.' Describes the old man's return and conversation.
  • 汝饥，可随手取松子橡栗食之 - Literal: 'If you are hungry, you can take pine nuts and acorns at will.' Gives advice.
  • 食后，体觉轻健 - Literal: 'After eating, the body felt light and healthy.' Describes the effect of the food.
  • 先是，曹常病寒，至是病减八九 - Literal: 'Previously, Cao often suffered from cold, but now his illness has reduced by eight or nine parts.' Describes Cao's improvement.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 猿洞 - A cave inhabited by monkeys, which is a common setting in Chinese folklore and literature.
  • 虎 - Tigers are often symbolic in Chinese culture, representing both danger and power.
  • 河洛二图、六十四卦 - The two diagrams of the Yellow River and Luo River, and the sixty-four hexagrams of the I Ching (Book of Changes), are significant in Chinese philosophy and divination.
  • 松子橡栗 - Pine nuts and acorns are traditional foods in Chinese culture, often associated with longevity and health.

Proper Nouns

  • 可将错就错 - Pragmatic approach to dealing with a discrepancy; 'accept the mistake and gain this extra profit.'
  • 懊恼无已 - Emotional state of extreme disappointment; 'extremely懊恼.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 银 - Common medium of exchange in ancient China.
  • 纹银 - Standard form of currency, fine silver.
  • 假银 - Fake silver, often made of lead or other metals, used to deceive.
  • 店主人 - Owner of a store, a common figure in ancient Chinese society.
  • 邻 - Neighbor, indicating a local community structure.
  • 门向 - Direction of a door, important for navigation in ancient China.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '主人接其银称之，十一两零三钱' is clear and straightforward, describing the act of weighing the silver.
  • The sentence '疑其子发信时匆匆未检，故信上只言十两' introduces a discrepancy between the actual amount and the amount stated in the letter.
  • The sentence '老人又不能自称，可将错就错，获此余利' presents a pragmatic solution to the discrepancy.
  • The sentence '遽以九千钱与之' describes the immediate action taken by the store owner.
  • The sentence '时价纹银十两，例兑钱九千' provides the exchange rate context.
  • The sentence '翁负钱去' describes the outcome of the transaction.
  • The sentence '一客笑于旁曰' introduces a second character who offers commentary.
  • The sentence '店主人得毋受欺乎' expresses concern about the store owner being deceived.
  • The sentence '此老翁者积年骗棍用假银者也' reveals the old man's deceptive nature.
  • The sentence '我见其来换钱，已为主人忧' shows the guest's concern.
  • The sentence '因此老在店，故未敢明言' explains the guest's hesitation.
  • The sentence '店主惊，剪其银，果铅胎' describes the discovery of the fraud.
  • The sentence '懊恼无已' conveys the store owner's emotional state.
  • The sentence '再四谢客，且询此翁居址' shows the store owner's gratitude and desire for further action.
  • The sentence '翁住某年，离此十里余，君追之犹能及之' provides the old man's location and the possibility of pursuit.
  • The sentence '但我翁邻也，使翁知我破其法，将仇我' reveals the guest's personal stake in the matter.
  • The sentence '请告君以彼之门向，而自往追之' suggests the guest's willingness to act.
  • The sentence '店主人必欲与俱' shows the store owner's determination.
  • The sentence '君但偕行至彼地，君告我以彼门向，君即脱去' outlines the plan.
  • The sentence '则老人不知是君所道，何仇这有' explains the rationale behind the plan.
  • The sentence '客犹不肯，乃酬以三金' shows the store owner's attempt to persuade the guest.
  • The sentence '客若为不得已而强行者' describes the guest's final decision.

  • 姓王名紫珍 - A standard classical naming structure meaning 'surnamed Wang, named Zi Zhen.'
  • 尤有神通 - 'Especially possesses supernatural powers,' with '神通' meaning supernatural abilities or spiritual powers.
  • 初烹時 - 'At the beginning of the brewing,' referring to the initial stage of boiling tea.

  • 此混沌象也 - 'This is the image of chaos' or 'This represents the state of chaos,' where '混沌' refers to the primordial formless state of the universe.
  • 少顷 - A classical term meaning 'a short while later' or 'after a while.'

  • 十二万年，不过如此一霎耳 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'Twelve million years is no more than a single moment,' emphasizing the vastness of time in contrast to a brief instant.

  • 总督河道时 - 'When he was the governor-general of river management,' where '河道' refers to river management or waterways.
  • 贾常在署中 - 'Jia was often in the office,' with '署中' meaning 'within the office' or 'in the governor’s residence.'
  • 人多崇奉之 - 'Many people revered him,' with '崇奉' meaning to revere or venerate.
  • 有不相敬者 - 'Those who did not respect him,' where '不相敬' means 'did not show respect.'
  • 贾必拉至无人之处 - 'Jia would take them to a secluded place,' where '拉至' means to take or drag to.
  • 将其生平隐事妻子所不知者一一语之 - 'He would tell them all the hidden matters of their lives that even their wives did not know,' with '隐事' meaning hidden or secret matters.
  • 其人愧服乃已 - 'The person would be filled with shame and submit, and only then would it end,' with '愧服' meaning to be shamed into submission.
  • 常问人 - 'Frequently asked people,' where '常问' is a common expression for 'often asked.'
  • 可畏鬼否 - 'Are you afraid of ghosts?' with '畏' meaning to fear or revere.
  • 曰畏鬼便已 - 'If you say you are afraid of ghosts, then that is already the case,' with '便已' meaning 'then it is already so.'
  • 如云不畏 - 'If you say you are not afraid,' with '如云' meaning 'if you say' or 'if you claim.'
  • 则是夜必有奇形恶状者入房作闹 - 'Then that night, there will certainly be someone of strange and evil appearance entering the room to cause disturbance,' with '奇形恶状' meaning 'strange and evil form' or 'weird and sinister appearance.'

  • 道人 - A term referring to Daoist practitioners, hermits, or spiritual figures in classical Chinese society.
  • 紫珍 - The name 'Zi Zhen' is likely a fictional or traditional name, not a known historical figure, and should be retained as is.
  • 嵇文敏公 - Likely refers to Ji Wenmin (嵇文敏), a historical figure, possibly a scholar or official from the Qing dynasty. The title '文敏' is a posthumous honorific meaning 'cultivated and sensitive' or 'cultured and perceptive.'
  • 总督河道 - Refers to the official position of 'governor-general of river management,' a role in Chinese bureaucracy responsible for managing waterways, flood control, and irrigation systems.
  • 署中 - Refers to the official’s office or residence, typically used in classical Chinese to denote a government or administrative location.
  • 崇奉 - A term indicating veneration or reverence, often used in classical texts to describe the public’s attitude toward a respected individual.
  • 隐事 - Refers to hidden or secret matters, often used in classical literature to describe personal or private information.
  • 奇形恶状 - A metaphorical expression for strange or terrifying appearances, often used in classical Chinese to describe supernatural or ghostly figures.

  • 至七日，某人必死，遍体无伤，而其软如绵 - Describes the effects of the curse, with the cursed person dying after seven days, with no visible wounds, but their body soft like cotton.
  • 但能魇黎人，不能害汉人 - Indicates the witch's limitations, able to harm only the Li people, not the Han people.
  • 受其害者擒之鸣官 - Describes the response to the witch's actions, those who are harmed by her capture her and report to the authorities.
  • 必先用长竹筒穿索扣其颈项下，曳之而行 - Describes the method of capturing the witch, using a long bamboo tube with a rope tied around her neck and dragging her away.
  • 否则近其身必为所禁魇矣 - Warns of the danger of getting close to the witch, approaching her will surely result in being cursed.
  • 不禁魇人，则过期已身必死 - Describes the witch's fate if she does not perform her rituals, if she does not curse someone, she will die herself after the deadline.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 黎人 - The Li people are an ethnic group in southern China, particularly in Hainan Province.
  • 熟黎 - The civilized Li people who have integrated into the broader Chinese society.
  • 生黎 - The wild Li people who live in remote areas and maintain their traditional ways.
  • 禁魇婆 - A witch who uses magic to harm others, specifically targeting the Li people.
  • 热河 - Rehe is a historical name for the region around present-day Chengde, Hebei Province, which was a summer capital of the Qing dynasty.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 婆中有年少者，及笄便能作法，盖祖传也 - Female shamans or spiritual practitioners; '及笄' means a woman has reached adulthood; '作法' means to perform rituals or magic; '盖祖传也' suggests that the ability is inherited from ancestors.
  • 其咒语甚秘，虽杖杀之，不肯告人 - '咒语' refers to incantations; '杖杀之' means to be beaten to death; '不肯告人' indicates that even under extreme duress, the secret remains unspoken.
  • 有禁魇婆，无禁魇公，其术传女不传男 - '禁魇' refers to the practice of exorcism or binding malevolent spirits; '传女不传男' means the practice is passed down to daughters but not sons.
  • 黎民买卖田土，无文契票约，但用笔签一片 - '黎民' refers to common people; '文契票约' means written contracts or agreements; '笔签' refers to a piece of paper or a slip used as a contract.
  • 售价若干，用刀划数目于签上，对劈为二，买者卖者各执其半以为信 - '售价若干' means the price is unspecified; '刀划数目于签上' means to mark the price on the slip with a knife; '对劈为二' means to split the slip in half; '各执其半以为信' means each party holds one half as proof.
  • 日久转卖，则取原主之半签合而验之 - '日久转卖' means after a long time, the property is resold; '取原主之半签合而验之' means to retrieve the original owner's half of the slip and verify it by joining the two halves.
  • 其税签如税契，请官用印于纸，封其竹签之尾，春秋纳粮，较内地加丰焉 - '税签' refers to a tax slip; '税契' means a tax contract; '请官用印于纸' means to have the official stamp the paper; '封其竹签之尾' means to seal the end of the bamboo slip; '春秋纳粮' means to pay taxes in the spring and autumn; '较内地加丰焉' means the taxes are higher compared to inland areas.
  • 黎人进舍黎民婚嫁，不用舆马，吉日，新郎以红布一匹往岳家裹新妇，负背上而归 - '进舍' refers to the practice of the groom visiting the bride's family before the wedding; '舆马' means carriages and horses; '吉日' means a favorable day; '红布一匹' means a piece of red cloth; '裹新妇' means to wrap the bride; '负背上而归' means to carry the bride on the back.
  • 若能生子而后负妇者，则群以为荣 - '若能生子而后负妇者' means if the couple has a child before the wedding; '则群以为荣' means the community considers this honorable.
  • 邻里交贺，各以白纸封番钱几元，至其门首，抛竹筐中 - '邻里交贺' means neighbors congratulate the couple; '各以白纸封番钱几元' means each neighbor gives a wrapped amount of money; '至其门首，抛竹筐中' means they throw the wrapped money into a bamboo basket at the couple's door.
  • 其主人以大瓮贮酒陈于门前，瓮内插细竹筒数条 - '其主人' means the couple; '以大瓮贮酒' means to store wine in a large jar; '陈于门前' means to place it in front of the door; '瓮内插细竹筒数条' means to insert several thin bamboo tubes into the jar.
  • 贺客至，各伏筒瓮而饮 - '贺客至' means the guests arrive; '各伏筒瓮而饮' means each guest drinks from the tubes in the jar.
  • 饮毕，又无迎送拜跪之礼 - '饮毕' means after drinking; '又无迎送拜跪之礼' means there is no formal welcoming, sending off, or bowing ceremony.
  • 余在肇庆府署中，州刺史陈桂轩为余言 - '余在肇庆府署中' means the narrator was in the governor's office in Zhaoqing; '州刺史陈桂轩为余言' means the prefect of Xizhou, Chen Guixuan, told the narrator.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 婆 - Refers to female shamans or spiritual practitioners in traditional Chinese society.

  • 税签 - A historical method of taxation where a slip of paper or bamboo was used to record tax obligations.
  • 舆马 - Traditional modes of transportation in ancient China, often used in weddings and other formal occasions.
  • 红布 - Red cloth, often used in traditional Chinese weddings as a symbol of good luck and happiness.
  • 肇庆府 - An administrative region in Guangdong Province, historically significant in Chinese history.
  • 陈桂轩 - A historical figure, the prefect of Xizhou, who provided the narrator with information.

  • 蚺蛇大如车轮 - Describes a large snake as big as a cart wheel.

  • 闻腥气知蛇至，先以妇裤掷去，蛇举头入裤吮嗅不已 - Describes how locals use the strong odor to detect the snake and throw a woman's pants to distract it.

  • 縣官萬公 - A title for a county official, likely 'County Magistrate Wan Gong.' '縣官' refers to a county official, and '萬公' is a respectful address meaning 'Mr. Wan' or 'Official Wan.'
  • 申詳立案 - A bureaucratic phrase meaning 'submit for formal record and file the case.'
  • 驗壽子、篙工兩尸 - 'Examined the bodies of Shousi and the bamboo pole worker (gāngōng).' '驗' is to examine or inspect; '尸' is corpse; '兩尸' is two corpses.
  • 細眼穿喉胸二處 - 'A narrow slit through the throat and chest area.' '細眼' could refer to a small slit or wound; '穿喉胸' is to pierce the throat and chest.
  • 買棺殓埋 - 'Bought a coffin, prepared the body, and buried it.' '殓' is to prepare the body for burial; '埋' is to bury.
  • 在船中不便医治 - 'It was inconvenient to treat him on the boat.' '医治' is to treat or cure.
  • 撐船至大王廟停泊 - 'Paddled the boat to the Great King Temple and moored.' '撐船' is to row or paddle a boat.
  • 扛祝升入廟 - 'Carried Zhu up into the temple.' '扛' is to carry; '升入' is to ascend into.
  • 言畢，食粥一碗，仍氣絕矣 - 'After saying that, he ate a bowl of porridge, and then died.' '言畢' is after speaking; '食粥' is to eat porridge; '氣絕' is to lose breath, die.
  • 是年冬 - 'That winter,' referring to the same year as the event.
  • 余同劉霞裳游沐陽 - 'I traveled with Liu Xichang to Muyang.' '余' is the first-person pronoun 'I'; '同' means with; '游' is to travel or tour.
  • 過劉家堡，泊船大王廟 - 'Passed through Liu’s Fort, and moored the boat at the Great King Temple.'
  • 往看諸神，皆寻常金裝木偶 - 'I went to look at the deities, who were all ordinary golden-painted wooden statues.' '金裝' is gilded or gold-plated; '木偶' is wooden statue or puppet.
  • 無他靈異 - 'No other supernatural phenomena.' '靈異' is supernatural or miraculous.
  • 劉向神問 - 'Liu asked the deity.' '向' is to face or address.
  • 有何惡而犯天诛 - 'What evil did he commit to be punished by heavenly retribution?' '天诛' is heavenly punishment or divine retribution.
  • 痴秀才 - 'Foolish scholar' or 'foolish literatus.' A term of endearment or teasing.
  • 民可使由之，不可使知之 - A reference to Confucius’s saying: 'The people can be made to follow a path, but cannot be made to understand it.' From the Analects.
  • 幽明一理 - 'The principles of the seen and unseen are one.' '幽' is the unseen or otherworldly; '明' is the seen or worldly.
  • 何必對神饒舌耶 - 'Why bother speaking to the gods at length?' '饒舌' is to talk at length or unnecessarily.
  • 水精孝廉 - 'Water Spirit Scholar-official' or 'Water Crystal Scholar-official.' Likely a title or nickname for a person, possibly a literary or colloquial term.
  • 童時誤入蛇腹 - 'As a child, he mistakenly entered the belly of a snake.' '誤入' is to mistakenly enter; '蛇腹' is the belly of a snake.
  • 黑无所见，但闻腥气 - 'It was black and he saw nothing, but only smelled the stench.' '黑' is darkness; '无所见' is saw nothing; '腥气' is a foul or fishy smell.
  • 扪其壁，滑氵达不可近 - 'He touched the walls, which were slippery and ran with water, making it impossible to approach.' '滑氵达' is a compound word meaning slippery and flowing with water; '不可近' is impossible to approach.

'孝廉' is a traditional Confucian title for a person of moral integrity and scholarly ability, often used in the Han dynasty for candidates for official positions. '水精' may be a metaphor or nickname, possibly meaning 'water crystal' or 'clear as water,' symbolizing purity or clarity.
  • 民可使由之，不可使知之 - A reference to Confucius’s Analects (2.2), where he says: 'The people can be made to follow a path, but cannot be made to understand it.' This is a philosophical statement on governance.

  • 季夏日正午，立埂上乘凉，见东池忽有一道浮沤，阔尺许，似潮涌来而氵砉氵砉有声 - Narrative about a specific time and place, describing a sudden appearance of a floating foam, literally 'On a summer afternoon at noon, standing on the dike to cool off, he saw a floating foam in the eastern pond, about a foot wide, like a tide surging with a sound.'

  • 顺问之邻人，云：“是水鬼移家也” - Dialogue where a neighbor explains the phenomenon, literally 'He asked the neighbors, who said, 'It is the water ghosts moving their home.''

  • 杭城仙林桥徐松年，开铜店 - Describes a person and his occupation, literally 'Xu Songnian, who opened a copper shop in Xianlin Bridge, Hangzhou City.'
  • 年三十二，骤得瘵疾 - Describes a sudden illness, literally 'At the age of thirty-two, he suddenly contracted tuberculosis.'
  • 越数月，疾渐剧，其妻泣谓曰：“我有两儿俱幼，君或不讳，我不能抚，我愿祷于神，以寿借君。君当抚儿，待其长娶媳，可以成家，君不必再娶矣” - Dialogue where the wife expresses her willingness to sacrifice her life for her husband, literally 'After several months, the disease worsened, and his wife cried and said, 'I have two young sons. If you do not survive, I cannot take care of them. I wish to pray to the gods to give me my life in exchange for yours.   • 夫许之，妇投词于城隍，再祷于家神，妇疾渐作，夫疾渐瘳，浃岁而卒 - Describes the outcome of the wife's sacrifice, literally 'The husband agreed, and the wife submitted a petition to the City God and prayed again to the family gods. The wife's disease gradually worsened, while the husband's disease gradually improved. She died after a year.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 有条大喜，讽诵不辍 - 有条 (Yǒu Tiáo) is a proper noun, likely a person's name. 大喜 means 'great joy,' and 讽诵不辍 means 'reciting without interruption.' This phrase describes someone who is deeply engrossed in reciting something, possibly a text or poem.
  • 其父俗贾也 - 俗贾 means 'a common merchant' or 'a man of the merchant class.' This indicates that the father is a typical businessman, not of noble or scholarly status.
  • 迫之赴吴门贸易 - 迫之 means 'forced him' or 'urged him,' and 赴吴门贸易 means 'to go to Wumen for trade.' Wumen (吴门) is a historical name for Suzhou, a major commercial hub in ancient China.
  • 有条郁郁而往 - 郁郁 describes a state of sadness or dejection. This phrase indicates that the person went reluctantly, feeling downcast.
  • 日赴市廛 - 市廛 (shì chán) refers to a market or commercial district. This phrase means 'going to the market every day.'
  • 夜仍阖户 - 阖户 means 'closing the door.' This phrase indicates that the person closed the door at night, possibly to study in private.
  • 隐身帷帐中，私自钻研 - 隐身 means 'hiding' or 'concealing oneself,' and 私自钻研 means 'studying on one's own.' This phrase describes the person studying in secret, hidden behind curtains or screens.
  • 越四月，疾亟而归 - 越四月 means 'after four months,' and 疾亟 means 'suddenly ill' or 'severely ill.' This phrase indicates that the person fell seriously ill after four months and returned home.
  • 时近重九，抵家遂卒 - 重九 refers to the Double Ninth Festival (重阳节), a traditional Chinese holiday. This phrase indicates that the person died shortly after returning home, close to the festival.
  • 柩停于家 - 柩 refers to a coffin, and 停于家 means 'left at home.' This phrase indicates that the coffin remained at home after the person's death.
  • 次年七夕前一日 - 七夕 refers to the Qixi Festival (七夕节), also known as the Chinese Valentine's Day. This phrase indicates that the event occurred the day before the Qixi Festival the following year.
  • 王睡梦中，闻内屋启门声 - 内屋 refers to an inner room or chamber. This phrase describes the person hearing the sound of a door opening in an inner room while asleep.

  • 见有条左手秉烛，右手执碗 - 秉烛 means 'holding a candle,' and 执碗 means 'holding a bowl.' This phrase describes the person seeing Yǒu Tiáo holding a candle in one hand and a bowl in the other.
  • 碗内腾腾热气 - 腾腾 describes rising steam or heat. This phrase indicates that the bowl contains hot food or drink.

  • 先生肚饥耶？特送点心来 - 肚饥 means 'hungry,' and 特送点心来 means 'especially brought some snacks.' This phrase is Yǒu Tiáo's question, asking if the person is hungry and offering snacks.
  • 王坐起接其碗，见内浮汤圆四个 - 汤圆 refers to glutinous rice balls, a traditional Chinese dessert. This phrase describes the person sitting up, taking the bowl, and seeing four glutinous rice balls floating in it.
  • 兼有铜铫 - 铜铫 (tóng diào) refers to a small copper pot or kettle. This phrase indicates that there is also a small copper pot in the bowl.
  • 遂忘其为鬼，竟挑食之 - 遂忘其为鬼 means 'forgot that he was a ghost,' and 竟挑食之 means 'ended up eating it.' This phrase describes the person forgetting that Yǒu Tiáo was a ghost and eating the food.
  • 及三而饱，尚留其一，随手交还有条 - 及三而饱 means 'after eating three,' and 尚留其一 means 'still leaving one.' This phrase describes the person eating three of the glutinous rice balls, leaving one, and handing it back to Yǒu Tiáo.
  • 有条复为下帐闭门而去 - 下帐 means 'lowering the curtain,' and 闭门而去 means 'closing the door and leaving.' This phrase describes Yǒu Tiáo lowering the curtain, closing the door, and leaving.
  • 王忽大悟，惊曰 - 大悟 means 'suddenly realized,' and 惊曰 means 'shocked and said.' This phrase describes the person suddenly realizing the truth and being shocked.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 陇西城隍神是美少年康熙间，陇西城隍塑黑面而髯者，貌颇威严，忽于乾隆间改塑像为美少年。
  • 或问庵僧，僧曰：‘闻之长老云，雍正七年，有谢某者，年甫二十，从其师在庙读书。夜间先生出外，谢步月吟诗。见一人来祷，乃隐于神后伺之，闻其祝云：‘今夜若偷物有获，必具三牲来献。’方知是贼也。心疑神乃聪明正直之人，岂可以牲牢动乎？次日，贼竟来还愿，生大不平，作文责之。神夜托梦于其师，将降生祸。师醒后问生，生抵赖。师怒，搜其箧，竟有责神之稿，怒而焚之。’

  • 吾即几上案中人也 - Literal: I am the person on the table; meaning: the woman who claims to be the victim of an injustice.
